title: Ahlfors Currents and Symplectic Non-Hyperbolicity
speaker: Spencere Cattalani
abstract: Complex lines are a class of pseudoholomorphic curves which generalize rational curves. Applications of complex lines to symplectic geometry have been proposed, but they remain poorly understood. In this talk, I will describe a framework for studying them based on Ahlfors currents. These currents allow a topological study of complex lines which is largely analogous to that of rational curves. As an application, I will discuss a construction of complex lines under only topological assumptions, generalizing a theorem of Bangert.

proof_stages.label: Motivation from torus non-hyperbolicity
proof_stages.summary: Classical rational-curve methods classify symplectic structures in rational and ruled settings but fail for the four-torus, motivating Bangert’s theorem on entire pseudoholomorphic maps and the search for a symplectic-topological explanation.

proof_stages.label: Ahlfors boundary-area mechanism and current definition
proof_stages.summary: Ahlfors’ conformal lemma supplies disks in an entire curve whose boundary length is negligible relative to area, so normalized pushforward integration currents have closed homological limits that can be paired and intersected with forms or cycles.

proof_stages.label: Equivalence of complex lines, Ahlfors currents, and disk inequalities
proof_stages.summary: Duval’s theorem and compactness identify existence of a complex line with existence of an Ahlfors current, equivalently with holomorphic disks satisfying arbitrarily strong linear reverse isoperimetric inequalities.

proof_stages.label: Constructing currents by disks and robust monotonicity
proof_stages.summary: For suitable closed symplectic manifolds, especially those retracting to large Lagrangian pieces in the universal cover and satisfying curvature or filling hypotheses, standard continuity arguments construct disks and a C^0-robust large-scale monotonicity estimate controls boundary length versus area, yielding Ahlfors currents and hence entire maps.

proof_stages.label: Homological restrictions via hyperbolic forms
proof_stages.summary: Forms whose lifts are exact with bounded primitive pair trivially with Ahlfors currents, giving control over the possible homology directions of the currents and producing applications such as the product-of-surfaces dichotomy.

proof_stages.label: Bubbling analogue for high-area low-genus curves
proof_stages.summary: Sequences of closed pseudoholomorphic curves whose area grows faster than genus have normalized subsequences converging to Ahlfors currents, with the proof using average curvature considerations and mixing of geodesic flow to turn global genus-area information into homological limits.

proof_stages.label: Moduli of Ahlfors currents
proof_stages.summary: Unlike finite-dimensional moduli spaces of closed pseudoholomorphic curves, the currents form an infinite-dimensional but well-behaved compact convex set, with compactness following from weak compactness and diagonal extraction and flexibility coming from disks.
